title: Admin API overexposes storage secrets and user API keys
summary: The legitimate admin API response exposes storage credentials and user API keys that are not necessary for routine dashboard access.
why_it_matters: Overexposed admin responses expand blast radius because a single authenticated request leaks reusable secrets for other systems and users.
manual_steps: Log in as admin through POST /login to obtain a valid admin token.
Review the response for infrastructure secrets and per-user API keys.
hint_ladder: Use a real admin token instead of the header-based admin panel shortcut.
Look inside the infrastructure object and each user record returned by /api/admin.
The flag is earned when a normal admin response includes both storage secrets and user API keys.
what_to_learn: Why legitimate admin access still needs least-privilege response shaping.
How to separate access control issues from sensitive-data-overexposure issues.
How a single authenticated response can become a pivot point into more systems.
remediation: Return only data required for the admin task being performed.
Remove storage credentials and reusable secrets from application responses.
Scope admin data views to minimal metadata rather than full secret-bearing records.
